label: PI 5
title: Pi 5
content: Using the Raspberry Pi 5 to explore a range of system level projects, including ad-blocking setups, understanding operating systems, and creating a portable coding environment. This device serves as a flexible platform for learning networking, system configuration, and expanding into more advanced hardware-based development.

label: PICO Microcontrollers
title: PICO Microcontrollers
content: Developing a microcontroller based system using the Raspberry Pi Pico to control a motorized mechanism that physically activates a button triggered camera for remote pet monitoring. The system enables remote interaction through a simple interface, focusing on hardware control, real-time input/output, and practical embedded system design.

label: Pi Zero
title: Pi Zero
content: Building a Raspberry Pi Zero based cyberdeck designed as a dedicated, portable device to run a custom .NET/C# dashboard for productivity, study tracking, and personal organization. The project emphasizes a focused, single-purpose system that integrates software and hardware into a compact, functional tool.

content: Conceptualizing an interactive, walkable digital museum experience that allows users to explore curated art and artifacts in a 3D environment
I’m currently experimenting with performance optimization and learning how image handling impacts real-world load times.
I recently began testing my website performance using Google PageSpeed Insights and noticed that large image sizes were significantly impacting load times, especially on mobile devices.
As a first step, I compressed my image assets across the site to reduce overall payload size and improve rendering performance.
I am currently redeploying these changes and will be testing again shortly to evaluate how effectively the optimizations improved loading speed and user experience.
This is something I am actively learning and refining as I continue developing the site.
